summary:
Set 029 Session 3 retires v0.14.2's global ~/.dabbler/current-orchestrator.json identity model and replaces it with per-session-set markers at <workspace>/docs/session-sets/<slug>/.dabbler/orchestrator.json (schema v3 with sessionSetSlug as an integrity field). Fixes the cross-window contamination defect (per memory project_consumer_repos - three parallel VS Code windows on three repos clobbered one global marker). Writer rewritten with a walk-up resolver that locates the workspace's single in-progress set; on zero/multiple in-progress or no docs/session-sets/ reachable, fail closed (skip the write, append a JSON entry to ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log, no orphan marker created). Reader updated to run the same walk-up algorithm; two watchers (state watcher on docs/session-sets/*/session-state.json + per-set marker watcher) keep the gauge in sync across close-outs and start_session events. SessionSetsModel.ts extracted (~131 LOC) as the canonical data layer; both the current native tree and the future S4 custom webview tree consume the same scan/bucket/sort/predicates. Self-protecting .gitignore (containing `*` then `!.gitignore`) dropped automatically into each per-set .dabbler/ directory so consumer repos inherit untracked-marker behavior without any operator intervention. Workspace-root .gitignore also patched in this canonical repo as belt-and-suspenders. Three verification rounds against Gemini Pro (gpt-5-4 returned 429 on the OpenAI Responses endpoint twice in a row including the smaller retry; the sticky rate-limit window outlasted the failed call, so cross-provider escape to Gemini was the right move): Round A writer + schema doc VERIFIED clean ($0.018); Round B reader + model + provider + tests + CHANGELOG returned MUST-FIX 3 ($0.047) - all three fixes applied (stricter slug-presence check using !== undefined rather than truthiness; vscode.workspace.onDidChangeWorkspaceFolders listener so the state watcher binds even when the view activates before a folder is open; output-channel logging on slug mismatch since the spec text says reader 'logs'); Round C confirmation VERIFIED ($0.020). Convergence not spiral per memory feedback_verifier_spiral_recruit_codex. Total S3 routed cost $0.085 against the $0.10-$0.30 forecast. Cumulative set cost $1.548 of $5.00 NTE. Version 0.14.2 -> 0.15.0 (minor bump per audit consensus Q9 - identity model change is breaking-within-the-v0.14.2-preview, but v0.14.2 never shipped to Marketplace so no external consumer is affected; the minor bump is the audit-trail-honest choice). New docs/orchestrator-marker-schema.md authoritative reference for the v3 marker shape, walk-up resolver, fail-closed posture, and multi-writer precedence (unchanged from v2). Playwright spec rewritten - 12 scenarios A-L: existing A-H updated to seed per-set markers and call startSession(seed, 1) so the resolver finds an in-progress set; new I (mismatched slug -> empty state), J (helper ambiguous -> write skipped + log), K (helper happy path verifies schema v3 + slug + .gitignore self-protect), L (helper outside docs/session-sets/ -> skip). Layer-2 unit tests repointed to import directly from SessionSetsModel (canonical home). TypeScript compiles clean; pytest 585 passed + 1 skipped (pre-existing ruamel ImportError on three migrate_router_config tests unrelated to S3); npm run test:unit 376 passing with 2 pre-existing unrelated failures (configEditor-foundation stub harness + notificationsSection). Process notes: the sticky-rate-limit observation on the OpenAI Responses endpoint adds to memory feedback_split_large_verification_bundles - shrinking the bundle doesn't help if the sticky window is still active; cross-provider escape is the right move. The self-protecting .gitignore design (*
!.gitignore
 inside each per-set .dabbler/) is a cleaner alternative to patching the workspace-root .gitignore at init time and was acknowledged by the Round-A verifier as 'a sufficient and elegant mitigation that satisfies the intent of the auto-patch requirement'.

reason.specifics: Session 4 is the custom-tree pivot - replaces the native dabblerSessionSets TreeView with a webview-rendered custom tree that embeds the v0.14.2 gauges as a per-row accordion body. GPT-5.4's pivot review flagged 14 row-context actions in package.json plus loading-state, scan-state gating, viewsWelcome integration, and ARIA tree semantics as the reimplementation surface - none deferrable. S4 is gated by its own pre-session audit (per memory feedback_audit_then_spec_for_substantial_features); the audit itself should reuse the Gemini-Pro escape pattern proven in S3 (router-driven Gemini Pro call + manual GPT-5.4 paste in GitHub Copilot per feedback_split_large_verification_bundles) given the sticky-rate-limit issue observed on the OpenAI Responses endpoint. SessionSetsModel.ts (shipped in this session) is the data-layer interface the audit will design against, and the future custom tree will consume directly. Opus 4.7 @ effort=high is the right choice: multi-file coherence at scale (webview message protocol + keyboard nav + focus management + 14 action handlers + ARIA tree semantics).
